blockingIssues.description:
When the winning assistant output spans multiple text blocks, `pickChronologicallyLastOutput()` rejoins them with `

` instead of preserving the message's literal concatenation. Providers such as Bedrock Camp preserve adjacent text blocks from a single assistant turn, while `extractLastAssistantText()` and `ScoopContext` still join those blocks with `''`, so outputs like `[text("Hello"), text(" world")]` surface as `Hello

 world` instead of `Hello world`.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to record that the current AgentBridge multi-text-block joiner (`\n\n`) is a known bug and that canonical assistant-text assembly elsewhere in SLICC still uses literal concatenation.

rejectedObservations.observation: The webapp-worker skill still does not explicitly document what to do when manual `agent` verification is provider-gated and no provider is configured.
rejectedObservations.reason: already captured in round-1 synthesis as a suggested guidance update
